title: Studying cancer drug resistance using BRCA-deficient mouse mammary tumor organoids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"18r4w","text":"Poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibition (PARPi) is a promising new therapeutic approach for the treatment of cancers that show homologous recombination deficiency (HRD). Despite the success of PARPi in targeting HRD in tumors that lack BRCA1 or BRCA2 function, drug resistance poses a major obstacle to PARPi efficacy. Several resistance mechanisms have been identified in cultured cells, but testing their relevance for in vivo resistance is cumbersome. As a novel approach to investigate these, we describe here the use of 3D cancer organoids derived from genetically engineered mouse models (GEMMs) for BRCA1/2-deficient cancers. Unlike conventional cell lines or mammospheres, organoid cultures can be efficiently derived and rapidly expanded in vitro. Orthotopically transplanted organoids give rise to mammary tumors that recapitulate the epithelial morphology and preserve the drug response of the original tumor. Importantly, GEMM tumor-derived organoids can be easily genetically modified providing a powerful tool for genetic studies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cno8d","text":"The Protocol described here is adapted from Sato et al., 2009; Sato et al., 2011; Kuo et al., 2012","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash tumor material in 10 ml PBS (in 50 ml tube)
Spin at 1200 rpm for 3 min and remove supernatant

Add collagenase solution (Advanced DMEM/F12 [AdDMEM/F12, Gibco] supplemented with 5% FBS [Gibco], 2 mg/mL collagenase type IV [Gibco], 2 mg/mL trypsin [Difco], 5 ug/mL gentamicin [Invitrogen] and 5 ug/mL insulin [Sigma]) and dissect tumor pieces with small scissors (dissect in petri dish and then transfer back to 50 ml tube)
Incubate in shaker at 37 oC for 30 min
Spin at 1500 rpm for 10 min
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend pellet in 10 ml ADDF+++ (AdDMEM/F12 supplemented with 1 M HEPES [Sigma], GlutaMAX [Invitrogen], penicillin/streptomycin [Gibco]) (with BSA coated pipette)
Spin at 1500 rpm for 10 min
Aspirate supernatant and add 4 ml DNAse solution (AdDMEM/F12 supplemented with 20 U/mL DNase [Roche])
Vortex for 3-5 min and add 6 ml ADDF+++
Spin at 1500 rpm for 10 min
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend pellet in 10 ml ADDF+++ (with BSA coated pipette)
Filter solution through a 70 um cell strainer into a BSA coated 50 ml tube and transfer into a BSA coated 15 ml falcon tube
Spin at 1500 rpm for 10 min

Perform several differential centrifugations (up to 4 times, depending on pellet size) • Aspirate supernatant • Resuspend pellet in 10 ml ADDF+++ (with BSA coated pipette) • Spin at 1500 rpm for 1 min

Resuspend pellet in Cultrex Reduced Growth Factor Basement Membrane Extract (BME) Type 2 (Trevigen, volume according to pellet size) mixed 1:1 with cold mouse mammary gland organoid medium (ENR medium, AdDMEM/F12+++ supplemented with B27 [Gibco], 125 uM N-acetyl-L-cysteine [Sigma], 50 ng/mL murine epidermal growth factor [EGF, Invitrogen], 10% Rspo1-conditioned medium and 10% Noggin-conditioned medium)
Seed on 24-well suspension plates (Greiner Bio-One) at 40 ul per well
Incubate at 37 oC for 30 min (or until BME has solidified) and add ENR medium at 0.5 ml per well
The medium is changed twice per week and organoids are passaged 1:4-1:6 once per week.
Carefully aspirate media without disturbing BME-embedded organoids
Place 2 ml cold ADDF+++ medium in 15 ml tube (on ice), take 1 ml using a P1000 pipette and use to collect BME+organoids
Pre-coat fire-polished glass Pasteur pipette (to tighten opening) with cold ADDF+++ medium before using it to disrupt organoids
Add 6 ml cold ADDF+++ media to each tube and spin at 900 rpm for 5 min
Remove supernatant, resuspend pellet in BME/ENR media mix and seed as described above
Incubate at 37 oC and add ENR medium
Carefully aspirate media and use 1 ml TrypLE to collect BME+organoids
Incubate at 37 oC for 10 min and use a pre-coated fire-polished glass Pasteur pipette to disrupt organoids
Add 4 ml cold ADDF+++ media to each tube and spin at 1200 rpm for 5 min
Remove supernatant, resuspend pellet in BME/ENR media mix and seed as described above
Incubate at 37 oC and add ENR medium

Organoid cultures were mixed with Recovery Cell Culture Freezing Medium (Gibco) and frozen following the standard procedures. When required, the organoids were thawed using standard thawing procedures, embedded in BME and cultured as described above.
Carefully aspirate media and disrupt organoids using TrypLE as described above
Count cell number, aliquot cell suspension into tubes and centrifuge at 1200 rpm for 5 min
Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet in viral suspension (diluted in ENR) supplemented with 8 μg/ml Polybrene (Millipore)
Transfer cell suspensions to a 24-well plate and centrifuge at 600 g for 60 min at room temperature
Incubate the plate at 37°C for 6 h
Transfer cell suspensions into tubes and centrifuge at 1200 rpm for 5 min
Discard the supernatant, resuspend pellet in BME/ENR media mix and seed as described above
The following day, add media supplemented with the appropriate antibiotic(s) for selection of transduced cells
For transplantation, use organoids at a size corresponding to an average of 150-200 cells per organoid
Carefully aspirate media without disturbing BME-embedded organoids
Place 2 ml cold ADDF+++ medium in 15 ml tube (on ice), take 1 ml using a P1000 pipette and use to collect BME+organoids
Take small aliquot, disrupt organoids using TrypLE as described above and determine cell number
Extrapolate cell count to organoid suspension, spin at 900 rpm for 5 min and resuspend appropriately in BME/ENR media mix: for KB1P(M) organoids – 10 4 /40 ul, for KB2P organoids – 5x10 4 /40 ul
Inject 40 ul of organoid suspension into the fourth mammary fat pad of recipient mice
None

Single Lgr5 stem cells build cryptvillus structures in vitro without a mesenchymal niche by Toshiro Sato, Robert G. Vries, Hugo J. Snippert, Marc van de Wetering, Nick Barker, Daniel E. Stange, Johan H. van Es, Arie Abo, Pekka Kujala, Peter J. Stange, Marc Ferrante, Robert G.J. Vries, Johan H. van Es, Stieneke van den Brink, Winan J. van Houdt, Apollo Pronk, Joost van Gorp, Peter D. Siersema, and Hans Clevers published in Gastroenterology on 12 Oct, 2017 https://dx.doi.org/10.1053/j.gastro.2011.07.050 Controlled gene expression in primary Lgr5 organoid cultures by Bon-Kyoung Koo, Daniel E Stange, Toshiro Sato, Wouter Karthaus, Henner F Farin, Meritxell Huch, Johan H van Es, and Hans Clevers published in Nature Methods on 12 Oct, 2017 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1802 10.1038/nmeth.4535
